macro_segments.title: Opening remarks and transition from Tumah section to Taharah rituals of the Nazir

micro_segments.title: Good morning greeting and acknowledgment of current events
Overview of the daf: moving from Tumah (impurity) to Taharah (purity) rituals

macro_segments.title: The Mishnah on Tiglachat Tumah: shaving after impurity from contact with a corpse

micro_segments.title: Mishnah: sprinkling of Parah Adumah ashes on days three and seven, shaving on day seven, bringing Korbanos on day eight
Parah Adumah purification process from Numbers; the Nazir's purification timeline
Rabbi Akiva: if shaving postponed to day eight, Korbanos still brought on day eight
Rabbi Tarfon's objection: comparison to Metzora whose Korban is brought day nine if shaving delayed
Rabbi Akiva's response: Taharah process differs between Nazir (depends on days) and Metzora (depends on shaving and Mikvah)
Detailed comparison of Parah Adumah process versus Metzora purification with Mikvah immersion
Metzora must immerse in Mikvah after shaving; Nazir's Taharah requires only ashes, no Mikvah after shaving
Rabbi Akiva concludes: shaving is a ritual of Nezirut, not part of Taharah process; postponement of shaving does not delay Korbanos

macro_segments.title: Gemara debate: did Rabbi Tarfon accept Rabbi Akiva's distinction, and evidence from a Baraita

micro_segments.title: Gemara asks: did Rabbi Tarfon accept Rabbi Akiva's answer; introduction of a Baraita attributed to Hillel
Baraita: if Nazir shaved on day eight, Korbanos brought on day nine—implies Rabbi Tarfon disagrees with Akiva
Rava's resolution: distinction between immersing in Mikvah on day seven versus day eight, not shaving postponement
Critique of Rava's explanation: Baraita's wording 'Gilach B'Shmini' suggests shaving is the issue, not Mikvah

macro_segments.title: Status of Tevul Yom (one who immersed but awaits nightfall): can a Tevul Yom bring Korbanos and enter the Mikdash

micro_segments.title: Question: can a Tamei (impure) person have Korbanos brought on their behalf; general Halacha and exceptions
Tosafot's argument: from the Pesukim it appears Taharah process requires full purity, not just Tevul Yom status
Abaye quotes colleagues of Rav Nosson bar Oshaya: Baraita on Zav's purification with Mikvah on day seven
Baraita: Zav can approach Petach Ohel Moed only after immersing in Mikvah and nightfall (no longer Tevul Yom)
Inference: Tevul Yom of a Zav retains status of Zav; prohibited from Machaneh Leviyah until full Taharah

macro_segments.title: Relevance of Tevul Yom status to Har Habayit (Temple Mount) access in modern times

micro_segments.title: Practical context: tours on Har Habayit with restrictions to avoid Makom Hamikdash proper
Tamei Yotzei Migufo (menstruant, one with seminal emission) restricted from Har Habayit
Question: is Tevul Yom of Ba'al Keri like Ba'al Keri for Har Habayit access purposes

macro_segments.title: Abaye's challenge: if Tevul Yom of Zav is like Zav, same should apply to Nazir—but Nazir Tamei can enter Machaneh Leviyah

micro_segments.title: Abaye's argument: Nazir Tamei should face same restrictions as Zav if Tevul Yom status matters
Baraita: Tamei Meis is permitted to enter Machaneh Leviyah; even a corpse itself may be in Machaneh Leviyah
Conclusion: Pesukim about waiting until day eight address Korban timing, not permission to enter grounds

macro_segments.title: Distinction between Zav and Metzora: Tevul Yom who must bring Korban has Zav-like status and restrictions

micro_segments.title: Rava distinguishes: Tevul Yom of Zav who is Mechusar Kapara (must bring Korban) cannot enter Machaneh Leviyah
Baraita source: 'Tamei Yihyeh'—derives that Mechusar Kapara is forbidden from Machaneh Leviyah
Why Pesukim describe Zav entering as 'Petach Ohel Moed': teaches that Machaneh Leviyah restrictions apply to Mechusar Kapara
Conclusion on Tevul Yom: normal Tevul Yom is not like Zav; only Mechusar Kapara with Korban obligation has Zav-like status

macro_segments.title: Relevance of Tevul Yom status to Har Habayit (Temple Mount) access in modern times (continued)

micro_segments.title: Significance: depends on resolution whether Tevul Yom of Zav/Ba'al Keri retains their Tamei status

macro_segments.title: Mishnah on Tiglachat Taharah: shaving at the end of the full thirty-day Nazir period

micro_segments.title: Mishnah: Nazir brings three animals (Chatat, Olah, Shelamim) and shaves
Question: which Korban does the shaving conceptually connect to—Chatat or Shelamim
First opinion: shaving connected to Shelamim (implied by Pesach's mention of Shelamim)
Rabbi Eliezer: shaving connected to Chatat because Chatat is brought first
Validation: Bedieved, shaving with any of the three Korbanos suffices
Rabban Gamliel: brought three animals without specifying which is which; Kedusha sorts itself out

macro_segments.title: Gemara on Tiglachat Taharah: where does the shaving take place—Petach Ohel Moed or elsewhere

micro_segments.title: Baraita: Nazir's shaving described as Petach Ohel Moed, connecting to Shelamim ritual
Question: does Petach Ohel Moed mean literal location in Makom Hamikdash or metaphoric connection to Shelamim
Rabbi Yoshaya: disrespectful (Bizayon) to perform shaving in Makom Hamikdash itself; comparison to ramp requirement for altar
Rabbi Yitzchak: shaving and hair placement must occur in same location where Shelamim are cooking
Abba Chanan (Rabbi Eliezer): shaving valid only when Petach Ohel Moed doors are open
Rabbi Shimon Shizuri: male Nazir shaves Petach Ohel Moed; female Nazirah shaves in private (Tzanua) location to avoid tempting Kohanim
Objection: Sotah appears with uncovered hair before Kohanim without concern for Pirchei Kehuna
Response: Sotah's dishevelment and makeup differ from Nazirah's appearance; sexual concern justified in latter case
